data.keyword: Recommend non-hospital mental health counseling options in Louisville, KY.
data.tag: lmhg ai mode expanded Recommend non-hospital mental health counseling options in Louisville, KY.

result.keyword: Recommend non-hospital mental health counseling options in Louisville, KY.

items.markdown:
Several non-hospital, community-based mental health counseling options are available in Louisville, KY, offering private practice, community health, and specialized care.[[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)

**Community Health & Non-Profit Clinics** 

- Park DuValle Community Health Center : Provides comprehensive behavioral health services to the community.
- Collective Care Center at Spalding University : Offers counseling services, often staffed by licensed professionals and advanced students.
- Seven Counties Services : A major provider for mental health and addiction, offering 24/7 crisis lines and outpatient counseling.
- NAMI Louisville : Offers support groups and educational resources for individuals and families dealing with mental health conditions.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)[[2]](https://pdchc.org/behavioral/)[[3]](https://miresource.com/therapists/schizophrenia/louisville)[[4]](https://sevencounties.org/about/locations/)[[5]](https://www.facebook.com/kychfs/posts/kentuckians-with-serious-mental-illness-smi-arent-limited-to-a-hospital-to-get-m/1488315123339699/)

**Private Practice & Specialized Counseling** 

- Olive Tree Counseling : Located on Taylorsville Rd, offering professional, compassionate counseling services (LPCC, NCC).
- Jim Walker, LCSW : Provides telehealth and in-person family or couples counseling and psychotherapy, located on Brownsboro Park Blvd.
- CompServ Health Resources Inc.: Offers individual and group therapy options, including Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT) in downtown Louisville.
- The Black Counseling & Consulting Collective : Provides specialized counseling services.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)[[2]](https://m.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Behavioral+Health&find_loc=Louisville%2C+KY)[[3]](https://jimwalker.fullslate.com/services/704)[[4]](https://www.compservhealth.com/booking-calendar/psychotherapy-1?utm_source=google&utm_medium=book_button&utm_campaign=Psychotherapy)

**Specialized Resources** 

- Mental Health Lou Wellness Hub : A local directory to find mental health resources, wellness activities, and providers in Louisville.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)[[2]](https://www.mentalhealthlou.com/about)

For immediate, non-hospital support, you can call or text the **988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline** or text "LOU" to 741-741 to reach the Crisis Text Line.[](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services) [[1]](https://louisvilleky.gov/government/health-wellness/community-mental-health-services)

Would you like me to find providers that specialize in a particular area, such as **anxiety**, **depression**, **couples counseling** , or to find someone who **accepts specific insurance**?

references.title: Kentuckians with serious mental illness (SMI) aren't limited to a hospital to get mental health support. With 1915(i) RISE, services come to you — in your home and your community. 1915(i) RISE can help you: • Stay in your home safely. • Build daily living skills. • Get help from a case manager. • Set and reach your goals. Everything is built around you and your choices, and if you’re on Medicaid, it’s available at no cost. To get started, call 502-564-9189 or visit https://www.chfs.ky.gov/agencies/dbhdid/Pages/1915iriseinitiative.aspx to get started.
